expectations.evidence: All 5 test classes have '@group group_ai_pm' in class-level PHPDoc. ProjectEntityCrudTest.php:11-13, AccessControlTest.php:11-13, CronAndQueueTest.php:11-13, SettingsFormTest.php:9-11, DashboardTest.php:8-11.
ProjectEntityCrudTest extends KernelTestBase (line 14). AccessControlTest extends KernelTestBase (line 14). Both CRUD and access control tests correctly use KernelTestBase, not BrowserTestBase.

All kernel tests include 'system', 'user', and 'group_ai_pm'. ProjectEntityCrudTest.php:19: ['system', 'user', 'group_ai_pm']. AccessControlTest.php:19: ['system', 'user', 'group_ai_pm']. CronAndQueueTest.php:19: ['system', 'user', 'group_ai_pm', 'queue'].
All 3 kernel test setUp() methods call installEntitySchema('project') and installEntitySchema('task'). ProjectEntityCrudTest.php:26-27, AccessControlTest.php:26-27, CronAndQueueTest.php:26-27.
All 3 kernel test setUp() methods call installEntitySchema('user'). ProjectEntityCrudTest.php:28, AccessControlTest.php:28, CronAndQueueTest.php:28.
All 3 kernel test setUp() methods call installConfig(['group_ai_pm']). ProjectEntityCrudTest.php:29, AccessControlTest.php:30, CronAndQueueTest.php:30.

Both functional tests declare $defaultTheme = 'starterkit_theme', not 'stark' or 'claro'. SettingsFormTest.php:17: "protected $defaultTheme = 'starterkit_theme';". DashboardTest.php:18: "protected $defaultTheme = 'starterkit_theme';". The expectation requires 'stark' or 'claro' specifically.

All setUp() methods in kernel tests call parent::setUp() as the first statement. ProjectEntityCrudTest.php:25, AccessControlTest.php:25, CronAndQueueTest.php:25. Functional tests have no setUp() method (inheriting parent behavior), so no violations.

Kernel tests in tests/src/Kernel/ (ProjectEntityCrudTest.php, AccessControlTest.php, CronAndQueueTest.php). Functional tests in tests/src/Functional/ (SettingsFormTest.php, DashboardTest.php). All paths confirmed by directory listing.

Kernel test namespaces: 'Drupal\Tests\group_ai_pm\Kernel' (all 3 kernel files, line 3). Functional test namespaces: 'Drupal\Tests\group_ai_pm\Functional' (both functional files, line 3). All match required pattern.

SettingsFormTest.php:45 '$user = $this->drupalCreateUser(["administer group_ai_pm"])' and line 46 '$this->drupalLogin($user)'. Pattern used in testSettingsFormAccess(), testSettingsFormSubmit(), testSettingsFormRetainsValues(), testSettingsFormDefaults().

SettingsFormTest.php:31 assertSession()->statusCodeEquals(403) for anonymous user and line 48 statusCodeEquals(200) for authorized admin. DashboardTest.php:32 statusCodeEquals(403) for anonymous user and line 43 statusCodeEquals(200) for authorized user. Both 403-deny and 200-allow cases verified.

No kernel test $modules array includes 'datetime'. ProjectEntityCrudTest.php:19: ['system', 'user', 'group_ai_pm']. AccessControlTest.php:19: ['system', 'user', 'group_ai_pm']. CronAndQueueTest.php:19: ['system', 'user', 'group_ai_pm', 'queue']. 'datetime' is absent from all three, despite Task having a due_date datetime field. This is the root cause of the rt-5 failure (missing 'options' module is a related symptom of incomplete $modules declaration).

CronAndQueueTest.php testCronQueueOverdueDetection(): creates Project and Task with past_date (lines 39-53), calls \Drupal::service('cron')->run() (line 63), asserts queue has items with assertGreater(0, $queue_count) (line 68). The full hook_cron -> queue pipeline is tested as required.

runtime_assertions.evidence: PASS — module re-enables cleanly after test files were added.

PASS — TESTS_FOUND:kernel=3,functional=2,unit=0,total=5. Correct distribution: 3 Kernel tests (ProjectEntityCrudTest, AccessControlTest, CronAndQueueTest) and 2 Functional tests (SettingsFormTest, DashboardTest).
PASS — all 5 files have no PHP syntax errors.
PASS — PHPUnit discovers 27 tests across all files. All test methods across the 5 test classes are discoverable.

FAIL — Kernel tests error: 'field_item:list_string plugin does not exist'. Root cause: missing 'options' module in $modules array across all kernel test classes. The list_string field type (used by Project status, Task status, Task priority fields) requires the 'options' module, which was not included.
NOT RUN — depends on rt-5 passing. Since kernel tests fail to execute due to missing 'options' module, actual test pass/fail results could not be verified.
